question.text: A trader buys a put option on TCS with strike Rs. 3,500 at premium Rs. 120. At expiry, TCS is at Rs. 3,600. What is the result?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Put Profit = max(X - S, 0) - Premium
Step-by-step: 1. X = 3,500, S = 3,600, P = 120 2. S > X, so put is OTM at expiry (no point exercising right to sell at 3,500 when market is 3,600) 3. Payoff = max(3,500 - 3,600, 0) = max(-100, 0) = Rs. 0 4. Loss = 0 - 120 = -Rs. 120
**The Trap:** Put becomes OTM when spot RISES above strike. Buyer loses entire premium.
**Cross-Exam Context:** Put buyer loses when stock goes UP (opposite of call buyer).
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Put OTM: S > X. Loss = premium paid. Put buyer needs stock to FALL.
